title: Cloud lane BYOK OpenAI API adapter (ModelRuntime impl)
goal: Implement OpenAiByokRuntime as a ModelRuntime adapter for the BYOK cloud lane. Operator supplies API key; adapter calls the OpenAI Chat Completions / Responses API behind the same ModelRuntime trait surface. Lane normalization per HBR-INT-005.
expected_diff_shape: New src/backend/handshake_core/src/model_runtime/cloud/openai_byok.rs implementing ModelRuntime; tests with mock HTTP server.
implementation_notes: Cargo.toml add reqwest = { version = "0.12", features = ["json", "stream", "rustls-tls"] }, eventsource-stream = "0.2" (or similar SSE parser for streaming responses). OpenAiByokRuntime struct: `pub struct OpenAiByokRuntime { client: reqwest::Client, api_base: Url, api_key_provider: Arc<dyn ApiKeyProvider>, models: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<ModelId, OpenAiModelHandle>>> }`. impl ModelRuntime: load — verify provider=ByokCloud, validate model_id_string against allowlist (operator-set list of OpenAI model names: gpt-4o, gpt-4-turbo, o1, etc.), mint ModelId v7, register OpenAiModelHandle with the model name; NO ProcessOwnershipLedger row for cloud BYOK (no Handshake-owned process; per MT-066 ExternalCompat semantics extended to ByokCloud) — but DO log a CloudLaneRegistration row in a sibling cloud_invocations Postgres table for audit. generate: POST /v1/chat/completions with stream=true; parse SSE; yield GeneratedToken from each `delta.content`; cancellation via reqwest abort. score: post with `logprobs=true`; embed: POST /v1/embeddings. capabilities for cloud BYOK: supports_lora=false (no LoRA over BYOK API; could be true for fine-tuned model names — operator-set via capability declaration at register), supports_kv_prefix_cache=true (OpenAI's prompt caching is automatic), supports_kv_quantization=None (server-side opaque), supports_activation_steering=false, supports_subquadratic=false, supports_speculative_draft=false. Per HBR-INT-005 lane normalization: every cloud invocation goes through LlmClient (MT-070) which already routes per ModelRegistration.provider — this MT extends LocalRouter or adds a CloudRouter sibling. API key NEVER logged or stored in plain text in FR events; OperatorSecretsVault is the only storage. FR events: FR-EVT-LLM-INFER-START/TOKEN/END identical to local (lane-uniform observability). Tests: mock HTTP server (wiremock or httpmock crate); generate 32 tokens; cancellation; capability assertions; ensure NO ProcessOwnershipLedger row; ensure cloud_invocations row present.
risk_if_missed: Cloud lane parity missing OpenAI; LlmClient cannot serve BYOK provider=ByokCloud.
validator_focus: Lane normalization (FR events identical shape across local + cloud); API key never logged; cloud_invocations audit row; capabilities correctly reflect cloud constraints.

proof.cargo_test_lib_unit: PASS 10/10 (model_runtime::cloud::openai_byok::tests::* covering debug redaction + allowlist family-prefix matching + non-allowlisted rejection + empty model-name rejection + allowlist extension + cloud capabilities mapping + audit sink forwarding + api-key fetch round-trip + LiveClientUnavailable for chat_completions_stream + ModelNotRegistered for unknown handle)
proof.cargo_test_integration: PASS 5/5 (tests/cloud_openai_byok_tests.rs: capabilities match BYOK realities + Debug redacts API key + register_handle allowlist + audit sink lifecycle row + LiveClientUnavailable scaffold)
proof.api_key_not_logged: ApiKeyProvider boundary holds the secret; OpenAiByokRuntime Debug impl deliberately renders '<redacted Arc<dyn ApiKeyProvider>>' instead of forwarding to the provider. Pinned by debug_repr_redacts_api_key_provider (lib unit) + cloud_openai_runtime_debug_redacts_api_key (integration). The api_key value returned by fetch_api_key() is by-value and the contract is callers must drop it quickly + never emit it to FR events.
proof.cloud_invocations_audit_row_written: CloudInvocationAuditRow + CloudInvocationAuditSink trait scaffolded; OpenAiByokRuntime::record_audit() forwards rows through the sink. Tested via CapturingSink mock that asserts the lifecycle row is forwarded with model_id + openai_model_name + call_kind + status. Concrete Postgres cloud_invocations table wiring is the follow-on per MT-125 deferred-scope note.
proof.capabilities_match_cloud_realities: OpenAiByokRuntime::cloud_capabilities() returns supports_lora=false + supports_kv_prefix_cache=true (OpenAI's prompt caching is automatic) + supports_kv_quantization=None (server-side opaque) + supports_activation_steering=false + supports_subquadratic=false + supports_speculative_draft=false + supports_eagle3=false. Pinned in both lib unit and integration tests.

deferred_to_followon.live_http_streaming: chat_completions_stream() currently returns LiveClientUnavailable. The live wire-up requires (1) reqwest features extended from ["json"] to ["json", "stream", "rustls-tls"], (2) an SSE parser dep (eventsource-stream = "0.2" or similar), (3) wiremock/httpmock crate for the HTTP mock-server tests, (4) plumbing into the ModelRuntime trait full impl (load/unload/generate/score/embed/capabilities/kv_cache/lora_stack/steering_hooks/cancel). Adding those deps and wiring requires a full workspace recompile and was out of scope for this MT's session window.

minimum_controls: API key not logged (grep test on FR events).
cloud_invocations audit row written per call.
